Off Calendar Jan-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom: Richard Sax, Esq. for Kieran Keaney (Petitioner). Jeffrey Loew, Esq. and Deborah Freeland, Esq. for Jessica Consoer (Objector/Petitioner). Andrew Schultz, Esq. for Andrea Leung (Successor Trustee) also via Zoom.

The Court addressed the matters on calendar today and thanked counsel for the joint case management conference statement. The Court stated that the petitions seem to be about the validity of a property agreement which is a contract not a probate document. Mr. Loew provided a brief update on the civil complaint stating that they believe everything should be heard together. The Court stated that it would abate the two pending petitions and have counsel come back after the civil complaint is heard. Mr. Loew stated that they have a settlement conference next week and that they are prepared to move as a civil action. Mr. Sax inquired about the amendment proceeding as a civil action under Probate Code 856.5. The Court confirmed that the amendment would proceed as a civil action. Mr. Loew noted that it was the same set of documents. The Court stated that the principle document is an agreement/a contract and not a testamentary document, that the two pending petitions would be abated, and that the parties should proceed with their settlement conference next week. Discussions conclude.

The Court orders the following:

All dates in the case management order are abated;
The trial date of March 21, 2023, is abated;
Status on the abatement or stay is set for July 11, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

Petition For (1) Order Authorizing And Directing Conveyance Or Transfer Of Real Property From Trust To Third Party Claimant, (2) Order To Quiet Title
Off Calendar Oct-11-2022 Continued To Jan-10-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Sax, via Zoom, for Kieran Keaney, Petitioner; Jeffrey Lowe and Deborah Freeland, via Zoom, for Jessica Consoer and Wesley Albright, Objectors.

The court confirms that the real property located on Crown St. is titled in the 2013 Trust and that the title of the real property on Raccoon St. is not titled. The court advises that the quiet title claim issue needs to be resolved in the civil division and not the probate division. There is a discussion regarding whether the 850 petitions are proper and what assets are trust assets. Counsel presented additional oral argument to support their positions with regard to their respective petitions. The court offers its position that any quiet title action should be filed in the civil division and advises counsel that the parties can proceed litigating their petitions in this court but without the quiet title issues. The court and counsel discuss the status of discovery and counsel confirms that they are amenable to participating in settlement discussions. The court advises counsel that this matter does not qualify for the pro bono mediation program, so it refers counsel to the court's ADR Administrator Elizbeth Kelber for discussions regarding possibly participating in the court's judicial settlement program - counsel is provided with Ms. Kelber's and the ADR Clerk's contact information. The court instructs counsel to meet and confer regarding amending their petitions so that the petitions can be streamlined for litigation in this Department.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Jan-10-2023 at 9:00 am. A Status of Amended Petitions is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Jun-28-2022 Continued To Oct-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Sax appeared by zoom for Kieran Keaney.
Jeffrey Loew appeared by zoom for Jessica Consoer.

Mr. Loew confirmed that there is an agreement to appoint Andrea Leung and stated that an amended stipulation was filed yesterday with a bond provision as requested by the examiner. The court informed counsel that notice of the 850 petition needs to be provided on the mandatory Judicial Counsel Form. Mr. Leow stated that all of the property should be held in the trust, that one of the properties is to be held as open space for a period of time for preservation and that there may be an issue as to who will choose who the board will be. The court stated that it does not sound like probate, that it sounds like corporate law, informed counsel that the Probate Department does not do quiet title actions and noted that the examiner's notes raised the issue as to whether this is an 850 petition. If this is an 850 petition, counsel is to make sure that proper notice is provided. The court informed counsel that it will issue a case management order setting future case management hearings and a trial date, that it will order a meet and confer, and that counsel is to make sure they follow the order. Counsel indicated that they need to do quite a bit of discovery and that they prefer to have a trial set for early next year. The Verified Petition for 1) Order to Quiet Title and Recover Trust Assets, etc., the Petition to Remove Trustee and Appoint Successor Trustee to Fill Vacancy, the Petition for Accounting, and the Petition for 1) Order Authorizing and Directing Conveyance or Transfer of Real Property from Trust to Third Party Claimant, etc. are continued to October 11, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Appearances: Richard Sax appeared by zoom for Kieran Keaney (Petitioner).

Mr. Lowe stated that he received confirmation that his objections have been filed, and that he has provided a courtesy copy to the Court. The Court informed Mr. Sax that it reads this differently than he does, stated that the Successor Trustees named in the trust instrument are Margaret, then Keith, that the first amendment of July 2020 deals with a different distribution, but not the Trustees and that the October 10, 2017 agreement doesn't confirm the requirements of the trust, so it thinks Margaret is the Trustee.
